open: Full 346-basket corpus render not run (budget) — only a 2-section, cap-primary-2 subset. Wall trend is directional (486.7s->269.6s on 2 sections, 45% faster) not a full-corpus number.

Kept/dropped verdict-set identity was NOT proven by count comparison: the two independent runs differ (control sec0 verified=2/dropped=2, treatment verified=1/dropped=2, etc.) due to writer/judge LLM nondeterminism across runs, not the code. Faithfulness rests on the STRUCTURAL argument (to_thread of the same verify call = verdict-identical by construction).

The coherence pass now takes ~29.9s per section (serial awaited to_thread per changed sentence) — no longer a loop FREEZE (0.55s gap proven) but a per-section throughput opportunity (batch/fan-out the re-verifies).

result.needs_fable: "For certifying these 4 verify-offloads as faithfulness-neutral before a full-corpus render: is the STRUCTURAL argument sufficient — i.e. `await asyncio.to_thread(verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify, x)` returns exactly what the sync call returns, verdict logic byte-untouched, so a two-run kept/dropped count diff (observed: control sec0 2/2 vs treatment 1/2) is pure writer-LLM nondeterminism and NOT evidence of a semantics change — OR does the gate require a deterministic mocked-judge, same-draft A/B proving byte-identical kept/dropped sets first? And gap-to-top: with concurrency now 2 at PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 and the coherence re-verify now ~30s serial-per-section, is the next lever lifting section-parallelism (4-8) or batching the coherence/sentence-repair verify fan-out within a section?"

result.gap_to_top: Two of four success criteria measured and met (loop-gap 0.55s < 2s where it froze 301s; two sections' phase-2 interleaving at concurrency 2). Remaining gap: (1) kept/dropped verdict-set identity to a serial control is asserted structurally but NOT measured — the observed cross-run count diff (2/2 vs 1/2) is plausibly writer nondeterminism but the mission gate demands demonstrated identity, and structural transparency does not cover the one new risk this fix introduces: verifier/judge shared-state races now that sections genuinely execute concurrently on threads; (2) the full 346-basket corpus render time was never taken — 486.7s→269.6s on a 2-section subset is directional only. Answer to Opus's question: the structural argument is sufficient for to_thread call-transparency, but NOT sufficient to close the gate; a deterministic same-draft, record/replay-judge A/B is required (it is cheap — zero LLM spend on replay — and it tests exactly the concurrency regime the fix created). On the throughput lever: lift section-parallelism (PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS 2→4-8) first, not coherence batching — the ~30s serial coherence pass overlaps across sections once parallelism rises, judge concurrency 16 / verify workers 8 have headroom, and 0 429s at 128-way is proven; batching the coherence fan-out is a second-order optimization to revisit only if the corpus render shows coherence as the critical path.
result.next_step: Add a record/replay shim to /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/scripts/_hb_probe_run.py: one recording pass captures every verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify judge request→response keyed by content hash plus canned writer_fn drafts; then replay the SAME drafts+judge answers through both arms — serial control (to_thread inlined) and treatment at PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 — and assert byte-identical kept/dropped sentence sets (this certifies the 4 offloads under real thread concurrency at zero LLM cost). On pass, set PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4 and launch the full 346-basket render via /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh with the heartbeat probe attached to take the top-line corpus number.

result.gap_to_top: Criteria 1-3 (concurrency>1, phase-2 loop-gap<2s, interleaving) are MET and the verdict-set identity is certified, but criterion 4 (full-corpus wall trending down) is not yet demonstrated: the baseline wall is still uncaptured (PROBE SUMMARY pending, render live at ~43min) and the wall is dominated by section 4's 274 baskets processed SERIALLY inside one to_thread worker (~9 LLM calls/min, in-flight~1) — confirmed both by log density AND by code structure: _compose_section_per_basket (verified_compose.py:3262) is a sync for-loop, one thread per section. The global 16-slot semaphore is only ~4-filled at peak, so the proposed 16->48 lever is mathematically null (nothing waits on it) — do NOT spend a 30+min run confirming it; two independent evidence lines (density ratio 3.9x matching 4-vs-1 sections, plus the serial loop in code) already constitute due diligence.
result.next_step: PIVOT — skip the 16-vs-48 A/B. (Passive: let monitor b89in0gb6 capture the baseline PROBE SUMMARY wall, then commit it + the 346->329 reconciliation to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.) Active: parallelize the per-basket loop in _compose_section_per_basket at /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py:3262 as MAP-THEN-REDUCE, flag-gated (e.g. PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS, default 1 => byte-identical serial path): run the expensive pure per-basket compose+verify+redraft in a bounded order-preserving ThreadPoolExecutor (the map), but keep the order-sensitive reduce — seen_spans/seen_texts/text_to_out_idx/seen_numbers_by_footprint dedup+UNION-consolidation at ~:3315-3330 — strictly serial in original basket order, because survivor selection there is order-dependent and parallelizing it would change kept/dropped verdicts. Certify byte-identical kept/dropped via the existing round-2 record/replay harness (workers=1 vs workers=8 arms), then take the timed solo-section-4 serial-vs-parallel A/B (repurpose scripts/_lever_ab_sec4.sh) as the trending-down lever, followed by a full-corpus rerun vs the baseline wall.
